title: Food Update
description: A new game-ready food balance and a flexible tool for DP-creators
body:
The purpose of this datapack is to make food more useful so that players eat more than just steaks and golden carrots. Main features: - Players gain additional effects when consuming food or water - Effects are divided between several food types - Non-stackable foods give long-lasting effects, stackable foods give short and weak effects Starting with Food Update 2.0, the default datapack ignores items with `custom_model_data` or `custom_data` to avoid preventing you from creating new food using datapacks. ### Huge update for 1.21.2+ Food Update 2.0 is here!
Don't agree with the balance? Create your own! A brand new preset system allows you to customize the effects of dozens of items in a single file. Minimum requirements to get started! You can even create an addon to easily share and manage presets you've written. Would you like to know [more](https://github.com/CapitanSuk/Food-Update/wiki)? Are you a food datapack creator? Use Food Update presets as a handy tool! Customize effects of custom food in a single file. Playing with mods? I'm not sure it's the best option, but Food Update doesn't have any restrictions on modded food. At least as far as Minecraft advancements allow. ### Other recommendations Food Update isn't balanced for vanilla potions, but I believe this datapack makes gameplay a little deeper. You can also download ["Amplified Potion Effects" datapack](https://modrinth.com/datapack/amplified-potion-effects) (1.20.5+) to enhance vanilla potions. If you've created an add-on for Food Update, please contact me so I can add a link to it. Please do not distribute add-ons as Food Updates with modified presets to avoid compatibility issues. ### Can I use this data pack with custom food? The default datapack is configured to not give effects for consuming custom food. However, you can add it to a preset yourself by following the wiki and configure unique effects distinct from the base item.
